sentence: Los nenes juegan afuera con la pelota nueva.
ipa: loh neneh xweɣaŋ afweɾa koŋ la pelota nweba
gloss_en: The kids play outside with the new ball.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per hualde2005.

sentence: Mi mamá compró arroz y habichuelas en el colmado.
ipa: mi mama kompɾo aros i aβitʃwelah eŋ el kolmaðo
gloss_en: My mom bought rice and beans at the corner store.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: ¿Vas a coger la guagua para ir al pueblo?
ipa: bah a koxeɾ la ɡaɡa paɾa iɾ al pweblo
gloss_en: Are you going to take the bus into town?
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); Romance rising diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je/we] per penny2002.

sentence: La playa estaba llena de gente el domingo.
ipa: la plaʝa estaβa ʝena de xente el dominɡo
gloss_en: The beach was full of people on Sunday.
notes: yeísmo: ⟨ll/y⟩ realised [ʝ] per hualde2005.

sentence: Ese muchacho canta bien bonito en la fiesta.
ipa: ese mutʃatʃo kanta bjeŋ bonito eŋ la fjesta
gloss_en: That boy sings really nicely at the party.
notes: word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ]; Romance rising diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je/we] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: El perro corre por la orilla del río.
ipa: el pero kore poɾ la oɾiʝa del rio
gloss_en: The dog runs along the riverbank.
notes: yeísmo: ⟨ll/y⟩ realised [ʝ]; alveolar trill [r] preserved per penny2002.

sentence: Compré diez chinas maduras en la plaza del mercado.
ipa: kompɾe djes tʃinah maðuɾah eŋ la plasa del meɾkaðo
gloss_en: I bought ten ripe oranges at the market square.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per hualde2005.

sentence: La luz del sol brilla sobre las montañas verdes.
ipa: la lus del sol bɾiʝa sobɾe lah montaɲah beɾdeh
gloss_en: The sunlight shines over the green mountains.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); seseo: ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: Mi abuelo cuenta historias del campo y la caña.
ipa: mi aβwelo kwenta istoɾjah del kampo i la kaɲa
gloss_en: My grandfather tells stories of the countryside and sugarcane.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); Romance rising diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je/we] per penny2002.

sentence: ¿Cuántas personas caben en aquella lancha?
ipa: kuantah peɾsonah kaβeŋ eŋ akeʝa lantʃa
gloss_en: How many people fit in that boat?
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per hualde2005.

sentence: El niño pequeño duerme en la hamaca del patio.
ipa: el niɲo pekeɲo dweɾme eŋ la amaka del patjo
gloss_en: The little boy sleeps in the courtyard hammock.
notes: word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ]; Romance rising diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je/we] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: No quiero que llueva sobre la parranda esta noche.
ipa: no kjeɾo ke ʝweba sobɾe la paranda esta notʃe
gloss_en: I don't want it to rain over the party tonight.
notes: yeísmo: ⟨ll/y⟩ realised [ʝ]; Romance rising diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je/we] per penny2002.

sentence: Los pescadores salen temprano buscando su pesca.
ipa: loh peskaðoɾeh saleŋ tempɾano buskando su peska
gloss_en: The fishermen set out early after their catch.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per hualde2005.

sentence: La mujer canta una plena alegre en la calle.
ipa: la muxeɾ kanta una plena aleɡɾe eŋ la kaʝe
gloss_en: The woman sings a lively plena in the street.
notes: word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ]; yeísmo: ⟨ll/y⟩ realised [ʝ] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: Dame un poco de agua bien fría, por favor.
ipa: dame uŋ poko de aɡa bjeŋ fɾia poɾ faboɾ
gloss_en: Give me a little very cold water, please.
notes: word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ]; Romance rising diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je/we] per penny2002.

sentence: El corazón del barrio late con tambor y baile.
ipa: el koɾasoŋ del barjo late koŋ tamboɾ i bajle
gloss_en: The heart of the neighborhood beats with drum and dance.
notes: word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ]; seseo: ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s] per hualde2005.

sentence: Aquella señora vende piraguas junto al parque.
ipa: akeʝa seɲoɾa bende piɾaɡah xunto al paɾke
gloss_en: That lady sells shaved-ice cones by the park.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); yeísmo: ⟨ll/y⟩ realised [ʝ] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: Buenos días, ¿cómo durmió usted por acá?
ipa: bwenoh diah komo duɾmio uste poɾ aka
gloss_en: Good morning, how did you sleep around here?
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); Romance rising diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je/we] per penny2002.

sentence: Las estrellas alumbran el mar durante la madrugada.
ipa: lah estɾeʝah alumbɾaŋ el maɾ duɾante la madɾuɣaða
gloss_en: The stars light up the sea in the early morning.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per hualde2005.

sentence: Mi hermano trabaja lejos, allá por la montaña.
ipa: mi eɾmano tɾaβaxa lexoh aʝa poɾ la montaɲa
gloss_en: My brother works far away, out by the mountain.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); yeísmo: ⟨ll/y⟩ realised [ʝ] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.
